TypeSignature.Value: .class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IOrderedQueryable implements class System.Collections.IEnumerable, class System.Linq.IQueryable
Public Interface IOrderedQueryable Implements IEnumerable, IQueryable
type IOrderedQueryable = interface interface IEnumerable interface IQueryable
public interface class IOrderedQueryable : System::Collections::IEnumerable, System::Linq::IQueryable

Docs.summary: Represents the result of a sorting operation.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable> interface is intended for implementation by query providers.
This interface represents the result of a sorting query that calls the method(s) <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy*>, <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending*>, <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenBy*> or <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenByDescending*>. When <xref:System.Linq.IQueryProvider.CreateQuery*> is called and passed an expression tree that represents a sorting query, the resulting <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable> object must be of a type that implements <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedQueryable>.
For more information about how to create your own LINQ provider, see [LINQ: Building an IQueryable Provider](https://learn.microsoft.com/archive/blogs/mattwar/linq-building-an-iqueryable-provider-part-i).
